steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best describes the overall structure of the passage, which moves from general context to a specific study to a finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It introduces a study of microbial communities near shipwrecks that has received significant scholarly attention, summarizes the results of that study, and then describes a research team's reaction to the study.: invents an emotional response the passage never shows. ✗
  • Choice B — It states the number of known shipwrecks, describes the historical significance of one of those shipwrecks, and then comments on the various microbes found at the shipwreck site.: mischaracterizes the passage's focus; the wreck is a case study, not the subject. ✗
  • Choice C — It names a famous historical shipwreck, describes the type of wood used to build that ship, and then explains how that wood type influences underwater microbial communities.: fabricates information not in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It notes a general scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, describes a specific study related to that interest, and then states one of the study's findings.: accurately tracks all three sequential elements of the passage. ✓
That matches Choice D — It notes a general scientific interest in shipwrecks' ecological effects, describes a specific study related to that interest, and then states one of the study's findings..
